published_prior_art.cite: CinelliHazlett2020/ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019/AdjahoChristensen2025 provide the component sensitivity geometries; the combined local support-function display is routine convex duality.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 is an orthogonal joint value-interval/non-rectangularity proposal, not a cross-language policy-score calibration frontier.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The witness says it refutes a splice that reuses a CH or variance-based scalar to rank policies under MSM/covariate-shift criteria, but it does not name a published paper or workflow that actually makes that splice.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Equal CH penalties do not imply CH robust-regret rankings tie unless the nominal regret difference is also fixed at zero; add theta^0_{pi1,pi0}=theta^0_{pi2,pi0} or state the claim only for penalties.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Patch Conjecture 2 by adding the missing nominal-regret equality condition, or rewrite the conclusion as a penalty-only separation and leave robust-regret ranking separation to Conjecture 1.

Either cite a concrete published calibration-transfer workflow that uses the refuted CH/R2-to-policy-ranking splice, or reframe Conjecture 2 as an internal sharpness lemma rather than an external negative-result target.
Update the Huang-Pimentel bibliography metadata to the current Biometrika publication record while keeping the arXiv history if the 2023 version is the intended comparison.

recommended_next_step: Revise Conjecture 2's ranking statement and target framing; the main Conjecture 1 novelty kernel otherwise survives both repo and published checks.
